preview_text:
The model favours Uruguay at 65%, with Saudi Arabia assigned a 11% chance of victory. The draw sits at 24%, making this a heavily asymmetric contest in which the underdog faces a steep climb to get a result.

The win-loss gap is substantial, but the 24% draw probability prevents this from being a one-sided forecast. Uruguay is clear favourite but not overwhelming; a significant slice of expected outcomes involve a stalemate rather than decisive victory. The match leans competitive enough to avoid a heavily lopsided scoreline prediction, though Uruguay enters with a decisive edge in the model's assessment.

Model and market are tightly aligned across all three outcomes. Sportsbook consensus places Uruguay at 67% (1.3 points higher than the model), Saudi Arabia at 12% (1.2 points higher), and draws at 21% (2.9 points lower). Polymarket prices track similarly. These gaps are negligible, indicating broad agreement on the probable shape of the match.
